I've been utilizing Afforai since November 2023, and it's been an indispensable asset in my quest for comprehensive insights. Whether delving into PDFs or navigating intricate technical topics, Afforai consistently delivers accurate and nuanced analyses. The platform's evolution has been impressive, with rapid developments and responsive support. What sets Afforai apart is its seamless integration of chatbots and diverse data sources, streamlining my research process. The inclusion of citations ensures the veracity of sourced information, instilling confidence in my findings. I'm confident that Afforai will continue to evolve, cementing its position as an essential tool for reliable and thorough information analysis.

•1 review

Afforai: Game Changer for Researcher Assistance It’s been a great help with my research and documentation work. The citation system and notes feature are fantastic and make organizing my work a breeze. However, there's some room for growth. One thing I'd love to see is more development on the API side, especially the ability to upload files. This would make the app even more versatile and useful for a wider range of tasks. Overall, Afforai shows a lot of promise, and I'm really excited to see what new features they’ll roll out in the future.

#### What's great

continuous updates (12)auto citation (11) [...] and customization of instructions, I was able to overcome initial limitations. This adaptability is a game-changer for tailoring your AI assistant to your specific needs. Enhanced Research Efficiency: For complex topics, Afforai significantly reduced the time I previously spent on research by nearly 80%. It not only helped streamline the research process but also helped filter out less reliable information found on unofficial social media channels. Exceptional Value: Afforai has been one of my best purchases in recent years. It has made my research more efficient, and its value is evident in the time and effort it saves. It's truly a "savior" for anyone engaged in research and knowledge management. Exciting Future Possibilities: The integration of Chat GPT-4 and future updates are eagerly

### The community submitted 6 reviews to tell us what they like about re:tune, what re:tune can do better, and more.

4.8

Based on 6 reviews

Review re:tune?

Re:tune is praised for its ease of use and robust functionality, making it a favorite among users for creating AI chatbots. The platform is noted for its seamless integration into workflows and excellent support. A maker review from 10xlaunch.ai highlights its superior API. Users appreciate the quick setup and deployment of chatbots, with features like the Restrictions and Capture tools enhancing its capabilities. While some users mention the need for more community engagement and automation features, the overall sentiment is positive, with many excited about its future developments.

Routine's approach is mind blowing and really well done. Great design, UX and feature set though some things are missing like Android, references etc. But everything seems to be on the roadmap. A few bugs remain as well but the team seems to be nearing a 1.0 version. [...] Report

Amazing product that I highly recommend. So beautiful, combines calendar, tasks & notes so that I don't need to 3 tools now. Oh and the dashboard functionality is just incredible. Still a few bugs here and there but it has become a lot more stable in the past 6 months. The new iOS app is also gorgeous!

results.title: Meta: Building the next evolution of digital connection. | Product Hunt
results.content:
Yibo

Decoding full sentences from non-invasive signals is wild, and I really like that you went the non-invasive route instead of chasing implants. Feels way more realistic for actual people to ever use. Curious who you picture reaching for this first, like folks who can't type easily, or a broader everyday crowd? What's the first real use case you're most excited to see people try?

Steven Snider
Jordan Gray
Airtop

Airtop

Do you mean to tell me that now I can prompt Claude to write my emails while remaining completely still?

Darly Selby

This feels like a major leap forward for brain computer interfaces. If accuracy keepsimproving, text input from thought could change accessibility tools completely.

Contentbuilder
MotionX
Rajdeep Mukherjee [...] Zac Zuo
Flowtica Scribe

Flowtica Scribe

Hi everyone!

Sure, the MEG scanner is still this massive non-portable machine. But the jump in performance is pretty big. Brain2Qwerty v2 gets to 61% word accuracy on average, and 78% for the best participant — where more than half the sentences only had one word wrong or less.

They basically went end-to-end from raw brain signals instead of using the usual hand-crafted pipelines, and it shows.  
  
Meta also released the full training code for v1 & v2, and the v1 dataset. The scaling laws look promising!  
  
Mind dictation soon?
